title: helianthus × scaberrimus var. subrhomboideus
intro: Helianthus × scaberrimus var. subrhomboideus, also known as the prairie sunflower, is a hybrid plant species within the sunflower family, characterized by its unique subrhomboideus leaf shape. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Helianthus × scaberrimus var. subrhomboideus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections.
